title: Neck mass ENT documentation

presenting_history.text: Supports documenting duration, fluctuation, infectious context, persistence, associated upper-aerodigestive features, and clinician concern in adults without asserting malignancy.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact adult neck-mass risk-history section supports onset, duration, progression, symptom impact, associated upper-aerodigestive features, relevant negatives, and clinician concern without asserting malignancy.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports documenting duration, fluctuation, infectious context, persistence, associated upper-aerodigestive features, and clinician concern in adults without asserting malignancy.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Evaluation of the Neck Mass in Adults

exact_section.heading: Key action statements 1–2 — suspicious history, duration, and risk stratification

examination.text: Supports clinician-performed examination of the oral cavity, oropharynx, laryngeal region, neck, and related mucosal sites when within competence; no finding is auto-populated.
examination.rationale: The exact targeted-examination section supports clinician-performed ENT, oral, throat, and neck examination without auto-populating a mass or mucosal finding.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-performed examination of the oral cavity, oropharynx, laryngeal region, neck, and related mucosal sites when within competence; no finding is auto-populated.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Evaluation of the Neck Mass in Adults

exact_section.heading: Key action statement 5 — targeted physical examination

investigations.text: Supports recording patient education, questions, planned follow-up, clinician concern, and already completed imaging or pathology context. Antibiotics, imaging, FNA, endoscopy, and referral decisions are not mapped.
investigations.rationale: The exact follow-up and education section supports concerns, reviewed specialist reports, clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without ordering imaging, FNA, endoscopy, or referral.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports recording patient education, questions, planned follow-up, clinician concern, and already completed imaging or pathology context. Antibiotics, imaging, FNA, endoscopy, and referral decisions are not mapped.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Evaluation of the Neck Mass in Adults

exact_section.heading: Key action statements 3, 4, and 6–10 — follow-up, education, and diagnostic workup context
exact_section.locator: official guideline key action statements 3, 4, and 6–10

referral.text: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.
referral.rationale: The exact NICE neck-lump and thyroid-lump recommendations were reviewed for age- and persistence-qualified cancer-recognition context; no automatic cancer or referral text was mapped.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.5 — laryngeal, oral, and thyroid cancer recognition features

source_limitations: Age, duration, site, size, mobility, tenderness, infection, smoking, alcohol, HPV, dental and thyroid context, examination, imaging, pathology, diagnosis, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
